mission.title: n8n: AWS Failed to Assume Role with AWS EKS Pod Identity
mission.description: AWS Failed to Assume Role with AWS EKS Pod Identity. Requested by 11+ users.

steps.title: Check current n8n deployment
steps.description:
Verify your n8n version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n n8n -l app.kubernetes.io/name=n8n
helm list -n n8n 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working n8n installation.

steps.title: Review n8n configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant n8n configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n n8n -l app.kubernetes.io/name=n8n
kubectl get configmap -n n8n -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=n8n
```
### Bug Description

Hello

I have a problem with AWS EKS Pod Identity.

steps.title: Apply the fix for AWS Failed to Assume Role with AWS EKS Pod Identity
steps.description:
## Summary
This PR is to fix the non-working EKS Pod Identity credentials resolver when using the system credentials option with AWS Credentials.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n n8n -l app.kubernetes.io/name=n8n
kubectl get events -n n8n --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "AWS Failed to Assume Role with AWS EKS Pod Identity" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
## Summary
This PR is to fix the non-working EKS Pod Identity credentials resolver when using the system credentials option with AWS Credentials.

#21584 introduced support for the `AWS_CONTAINER_AUTHORIZATION_TOKEN_FILE` required for EKS Pod Identity Authentication. There is an existing bug which predates #21584 which erroneously adds 'Bearer ' as a prefix to the Authorization header.
codeSnippets:

[ ] Please close this issue – I am no longer experiencing it

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with n8n installed or the issue environment reproducible.
